root_text.an3.66:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā nandako sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde.
root_text.an3.66:1.3: Atha kho sāḷho ca migāranattā sāṇo ca sekhuniyanattā yenāyasmā nandako tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ nandakaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho sāḷhaṁ migāranattāraṁ āyasmā nandako etadavoca:
root_text.an3.66:2.1: “Etha tumhe, sāḷhā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.66:2.2: Yadā tumhe, sāḷhā, attanāva jāneyyātha ‘ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, sāḷhā, pajaheyyātha.
root_text.an3.66:5.2: Luddho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, abhijjhālu pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:9.2: Duṭṭho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, byāpannacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:13.2: Mūḷho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, avijjāgato pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:15.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, sāḷhā, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an3.66:21.1: “Samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattanti, no vā?
root_text.an3.66:23.2: ‘etha tumhe, sāḷhā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.66:23.4: ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, sāḷhā, pajaheyyāthā’ti,
root_text.an3.66:23.5: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an3.66:24.1: Etha tumhe, sāḷhā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.66:24.3: ‘ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, sāḷhā, upasampajja vihareyyātha.
root_text.an3.66:25.1: Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, sāḷhā, atthi alobho”ti?
root_text.an3.66:27.2: Aluddho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, anabhijjhālu neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, parampi na tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:31.2: Aduṭṭho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, abyāpannacitto neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, parampi na tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:35.2: Amūḷho kho ayaṁ, sāḷhā, vijjāgato neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, parampi na tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.66:37.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, sāḷhā, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an3.66:43.1: “Samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattanti, no vā?
root_text.an3.66:45.2: ‘etha tumhe, sāḷhā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.66:45.4: ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, sāḷhā, upasampajja vihareyyāthā’ti,
root_text.an3.66:45.5: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an3.66:46.1: Sa kho so, sāḷhā, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno patissato mettāsahagatena cetasā …pe…
root_text.an3.66:46.4: upekkhāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ, iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ upekkhāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjhena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.an3.66:46.6: ‘atthi idaṁ, atthi hīnaṁ, atthi paṇītaṁ, atthi imassa saññāgatassa uttari nissaraṇan’ti.
root_text.an3.66:46.7: Tassa evaṁ jānato evaṁ passato kāmāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, bhavāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, avijjāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati;
root_text.an3.66:46.9: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an3.66:47.2: ‘ahu pubbe lobho, tadahu akusalaṁ, so etarahi natthi, iccetaṁ kusalaṁ;
root_text.an3.66:47.4: ahu pubbe moho, tadahu akusalaṁ, so etarahi natthi, iccetaṁ kusalan’ti.
root_text.an3.66:47.5: So diṭṭheva dhamme nicchāto nibbuto sītibhūto sukhappaṭisaṁvedī brahmabhūtena attanā viharatī”ti.

translation_text.an3.66:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Nandaka was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.an3.66:1.3: Then Sāḷha, Migāra’s grandson, and Rohaṇa, Pekhuṇiya’s grandson went up to Venerable Nandaka, bowed, and sat down to one side. Then Venerable Nandaka said to Sāḷha:
translation_text.an3.66:2.1: “Please, Sāḷha and friend, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think ‘The ascetic is our respected teacher.’
translation_text.an3.66:2.2: But when you know for yourselves: ‘These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering’, then you should give them up.
translation_text.an3.66:5.1: “‘Covetousness’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:5.2: A person who is greedy and covetous kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.66:9.1: “‘Malice’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:9.2: A hateful and malicious person kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.66:13.1: “‘Ignorance’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:13.2: A person who is deluded and ignorant kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.66:15.1: “What do you think, Sāḷha, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an3.66:21.1: “When you undertake them, do they lead to harm and suffering, or not?
translation_text.an3.66:21.2: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.66:22.1: “When you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering.
translation_text.an3.66:23.1: “So, Sāḷha and friend, when I said:
translation_text.an3.66:23.2: ‘Please, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an3.66:23.3: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.66:23.4: “These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering”, then you should give them up.’
translation_text.an3.66:23.5: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an3.66:24.1: Please, Sāḷha and friend, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think ‘The ascetic is our respected teacher.’
translation_text.an3.66:24.2: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.66:24.3: ‘These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness’, then you should acquire them and keep them.
translation_text.an3.66:25.1: What do you think? Is contentment real?”
translation_text.an3.66:27.1: “‘Satisfaction’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:27.2: A person who is content and satisfied doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit adultery, lie, or encourage others to do the same. Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an3.66:29.1: What do you think? Is love real?”
translation_text.an3.66:31.1: “‘Kindness’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:31.2: A loving and kind-hearted person doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit adultery, lie, or encourage others to do the same. Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an3.66:35.1: “‘Knowledge’ is what I mean by this.
translation_text.an3.66:35.2: A person who understands and knows doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit adultery, lie, or encourage others to do the same. Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an3.66:37.1: “What do you think, Sāḷha, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an3.66:43.1: “When you undertake them, do they lead to welfare and happiness, or not?
translation_text.an3.66:43.2: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.66:44.1: “When you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an3.66:45.1: “So, Sāḷha and friend, when I said:
translation_text.an3.66:45.2: ‘Please, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an3.66:45.3: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.66:45.4: “These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness”, then you should acquire them and keep them.’
translation_text.an3.66:45.5: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an3.66:46.1: Then that noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of love …
translation_text.an3.66:46.4: equanimity to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of equanimity to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an3.66:46.6: ‘There is this, there is what is worse than this, there is what is better than this, and there is an escape beyond the scope of perception.’
translation_text.an3.66:46.7: Knowing and seeing like this, their mind is freed from the defilements of sensuality, desire to be reborn, and ignorance.
translation_text.an3.66:46.8: When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.an3.66:46.9: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.an3.66:47.2: ‘Formerly there was greed, which was unskillful. Now there is none, so that’s skillful.
translation_text.an3.66:47.3: Formerly there was hate, which was unskillful. Now there is none, so that’s skillful.
translation_text.an3.66:47.4: Formerly there was delusion, which was unskillful. Now there is none, so that’s skillful.’
translation_text.an3.66:47.5: So they live without wishes in this very life, quenched, cooled, experiencing bliss, with self become divine.”

variant_text.an3.66:1.3: migāranattā sāṇo ca sekhuniyanattā → rohaṇo ca pekhuniyanattā (bj); rohano ca pekhuṇiyanattā (sya-all, km); rohaṇo ca pekhuṇiyanattā (pts1ed)
